iterations.title: Map cli.py's responsibilities and design the target structure
Resolve the forks and plan the refactor slices
E1: stand up lume/cli/ package; move cohesive helpers into submodules
E2: Context + per-verb handler registry; thin main()
E3: closeout - retro, done-when, lume close
P4: split handlers.py into a handlers/ package by verb group

plan.sketch: E1: stand up the lume/cli/ package and move the cohesive helpers into concern submodules (catalog.py: _pos/flag-consts/_verb/_CATALOG/_VERB_NAMES/_VERBS/USAGE; io.py: _ok/_fail/_code_for/_json_out + _render_detail/_render_queue + _detail_data/_queue_data/_iteration_summary; flags.py: _extract_flag/_extract_bool_flag; _ENTITY_KEY). cli/__init__.py re-exports main, _CATALOG, _VERB_NAMES. main() logic UNCHANGED, only imports move. DONE = full suite green with no assertion edits; lume.cli.main/_CATALOG/_VERB_NAMES importable; behaviour byte-identical.

E2: introduce a Context dataclass + a per-verb handler registry. Convert each verb's validation+action+output into a handler(ctx)->int (arg validation moved in from main's pre-pass); main() becomes parse -> build ctx -> registry lookup -> call. The if-chain is gone. DONE = main() is thin routing; every verb behaves byte-identically; suite green no assertion edits. Split by verb group if the diff is too large to review.
E3: closeout - retro (did the restructure buy back its cost? is a verb now local to add/change?), confirm main()/file structure improved + external contract unchanged, lume close.

P4: split handlers.py (~290 lines) into a handlers/ package by verb group (e.g. lifecycle, discovery, authoring); handlers/__init__ assembles HANDLERS + re-exports handle_transition so app's import is unchanged. Behaviour-preserving; suite green no assertion edits.
